rating_source: UK parent review aggregates for ANTILOP
age_raw: Able to sit unaided; up to 3 years / 15 kg
rank_rationale: #1: only fully verified buyable UK highchair primary on research day; overlaps band at sitting stage.
include_rationale: First tastes are getting close, but the chair only belongs when sitting is steady without help. ANTILOP keeps cleanup honest for porridge experiments. Budget for legs and tray as well as the shell on the same trip so you leave with a usable seat.

age_raw: From about 6 months to 15kg / approx 3 years
rank_rationale: #2: best fold feature set after verified budget ANTILOP.
include_rationale: When ANTILOP feels too bare, Mimzy adds fold and tray storage for small kitchens. Confirm live stock on Boots before ordering because retailer pages can soft block bots. Strong compact fold candidate in UK roundups for flats and narrow dining spaces.

age_raw: From 6 months with Baby Set (sold separately)
rank_rationale: #3: longevity champion after fold and budget paths.
include_rationale: If you want one chair for years, Tripp Trapp is the UK premium answer, with Baby Set extras in the budget. Not the first buy if weaning is weeks away and cash is tight. Confirm live Boots stock before you commit to the full set.

rank_rationale: #4: UK brand pattern option after budget/fold/premium trio.
include_rationale: When you want more colour than ANTILOP without Tripp Trapp money, Noodle is the UK pattern pick. Confirm Boots stock live; soft bot pages appeared during research. Still a clear chair for the sitting stage once independent sitting is reliable.

rank_rationale: #5: design premium slot; everyday needs covered by ranks 1-3.
include_rationale: More design led and pricier; only if the look and longevity come ahead of the budget path for a table seat. Confirm Boots stock live before ordering. Ranked last because ANTILOP, Mimzy and Tripp Trapp cover most Conor needs first for early tastes.
